message:
fix(reactor): apply events.len() consistency fix to LabReactor poll

Extend the reactor event-count fix (commits 5d74e64, 775ffdf) to the
LabReactor test/simulation backend, completing coverage across all five
reactor implementations (epoll, kqueue, macOS, Windows IOCP, Lab).

Changes:
- Add events.clear() at poll entry so stale events from a previous poll
  cycle are not carried over (matches Windows IOCP behavior)
- Remove manual `count` variable from the delivered_events tuple;
  return events.len() after pushing delivered events, ensuring the
  returned count always matches what the caller can actually observe
- Simplify the inner block to return only delivered_events (not a tuple)

Tests:
- poll_clears_existing_events_before_next_poll: verifies that a second
  poll with no new readiness returns 0 and an empty Events buffer
- poll_returns_stored_count_when_capacity_saturates: registers 2 tokens
  as ready but polls into a capacity-1 buffer, confirming count == 1
  matches the single stored event

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
